QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_11

question.text: Which is the correct order of the mutual fund structure from top to bottom?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The correct hierarchy is: (1) Sponsor sets up the trust and appoints trustees, (2) Trustees form a Trust to protect investors, (3) Trustees appoint the AMC to manage funds, (4) AMC works with Custodian (holds securities) and RTA (maintains records). Think of it as: who creates whom — Sponsor creates the Trust, Trust appoints the AMC, AMC works with Custodian and RTA. **The Trap:** The other sequences are jumbled. Remember the logical flow: someone has to set it up first (Sponsor), then someone has to oversee it (Trustees), then someone manages it (AMC), then service providers help (Custodian, RTA). **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under MF structure hierarchy. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use this memory trick: "S-T-A-C-R" — Sponsor, Trust, AMC, Custodian, RTA. Think of it as "STAR" with a C in the middle. This sequence comes up frequently in exams.
